elements.sent-2411-001: We define a Hopf algebra of polylogarithms of an infinite field, which is a candidate for a conjectural Hopf algebra of framed mixed Tate motives.
elements.sent-2411-002: Our definition is elementary and mimics Goncharov's construction of higher Bloch groups.
elements.sent-2411-003: We also discuss the Hodge and motivic realization of the Hopf algebra of polylogarithms.
elements.sent-2411-004: Multiple polylogarithms are multivalued functions of variables $x_1,\dots,x_k\in \mathbb{C}$ depending on positive integer parameters $n_1,\dots,n_k\in \mathbb{N}$.
elements.sent-2411-005: In the polydisc $|x_1|,|x_2|,\dots, |x_k| <1$ the multiple polylogarithm \( \Li_{n_1, \ldots, n_k \) of weight \( n = n_1 + \cdots + n_k \) and depth \( k \) is defined by the power series \begin{equation}\label{FormulaPolylogarithm} \Li_{n_1,n_2,\dots, n_k}(x_1,x_2,\dots,x_k)=\sum_{0<m_1<m_2<\dots<m_k}\frac{x_1^{m_1} x_2^{m_2}\dots x_k^{m_k}}{m_1^{n_1}m_2^{n_2}\dots m_k^{n_k}}\,.\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-006: Multiple polylogarithms satisfy numerous functional equations, like the following reflection equation for dilogarithm discovered by Euler: \begin{equation}\label{FormulaEulerEquation} \Li_2(a)+\Li_2(1-a)=\frac{\pi^2}{6}-\log(a) \log(1-a) \,, \quad \text{for $0<a<1$.}\end{equation} Working with identities between multivalued functions can be rather tedious: even the simple equation $\log(a_1)+\log(a_2)=\log(a_1a_2)$ only holds modulo multiples of $2\pi i$.
elements.sent-2411-007: It is not hard to see that the monodromy of a multiple polylogarithm of weight $n$ is a product of $2\pi i$ with multiple polylogarithms of weight $n-1$.
elements.sent-2411-008: Henceforth we will work modulo multiples of $2\pi i$.
elements.sent-2411-009: Goncharov conjectured that multiple polylogarithms are in direct connection with algebraic $K$-theory of fields; see \cite{Cle21 for an overview of Goncharov's programme.}
elements.sent-2411-010: More specifically, he conjectured that for a field $F$ there exists a graded connected commutative Hopf algebra $\mathcal{H _{\bullet ^{\M (F)$ of ``polylogarithms with arguments in $F$.'' It should be generated as a vector space over $\Q$ by formal symbols \begin{equation}\Li_{n_1,n_2,\dots, n_d}^{\M}(a_1,a_2,\dots,a_d)\in H_n^{\M}(F)\end{equation} for $a_1,\dots,a_d\in F$ and $n_1,\dots,n_d\in \mathbb{N}$, subject to certain relations which hold for polylogarithms as analytic functions modulo multiples of $2\pi i$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-011: For $n\geq 1$, denote by $[H^{i (\mathcal{H ^{\M (F),\Q)]_n$ the cohomology groups of the $n$-th graded component of the cobar complex \begin{equation}0 \lra H^{\M}_{n}(F)\lra \bigoplus_{\substack{n_1+n_2=n n_1,n_2\geq 1}}H^{\M}_{n_1}(F)\otimes H^{\M}_{n_2}(F) \lra \cdots\end{equation} of the Hopf algebra $\mathcal{H ^{\M _{\bullet (F).$ Goncharov conjectured that \begin{equation}\label{FormulaPolylogsKtheory} [H^{i}(\mathcal{H}_{\bullet}^{\M}(F),\Q)]_n \overset{?}{\cong} \gr_{n}^{\gamma} K_{2n-i}(F)_{\Q}\,,\end{equation} where $\gamma$ denotes the $\gamma$-filtration on the algebraic $K$-theory groups and subscript $\Q$ stands for the rationalization.}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-012: Our goal is to define a version $\mathcal{H _n(F)$ of a Hopf algebra of multiple polylogarithms $\mathcal{H _n^{\M (F)$ in an elementary and algebraic way.}}}

elements.sent-2411-395: Multiple polylogarithms satisfy numerous functional equations, like the following {\it reflection equation for dilogarithm discovered by Euler: \begin{equation}\label{FormulaEulerEquation} \Li_2(a)+\Li_2(1-a)=\frac{\pi^2}{6}-\log(a) \log(1-a) \,, \quad \text{for $0<a<1$.}\end{equation} Working with identities between multivalued functions can be rather tedious: even the simple equation $\log(a_1)+\log(a_2)=\log(a_1a_2)$ only holds modulo integer multiples of $2\pi i$.}

elements.sent-2411-453: In this and the following section we define the Hopf algebra of formal multiple polylogarithms $\mathcal{H ^\mathrm{f (F)$.}}
elements.sent-2411-454: We start with constructing its Lie coalgebra of indecomposables $\mathcal{L ^\mathrm{f (F)$.}}
elements.sent-2411-455: The Hopf algebra $\mathcal{H ^\mathrm{f (F)$ is defined as the universal coenveloping coalgebra $U^c\bigl(\mathcal{L ^\mathrm{f (F)\bigr)$ of $\mathcal{L ^\mathrm{f (F)$.}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-456: We will refer to the grading $n$ in $\mathcal{L ^\mathrm{f _n(F)$ and $\mathcal{H ^\mathrm{f _n(F)$ as the weight.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-457: For the case of a finite field $F=\mathbb{F _q$ we put $\mathcal{L ^\mathrm{f (\mathbb{F _q)=0$ (see \autoref{PropositionFrobenius for the rationale).}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-458: In the rest of the paper we assume that $F$ is infinite.
elements.sent-2411-459: Consider a $\Q$-vector space $\mathcal{A _n(F)$ for $n\geq 1$ spanned by \( \llp x_0,x_1,\ldots,x_n \rrp \), for all ordered tuples of points $(x_0,x_1,\dots,x_n)\in F^{n+1}$, modulo relations \begin{equation}\tag*{$\bf (A_1)$}\label{Acycle} \llp x_0,x_1,\dots, x_n \rrp = \llp x_1,x_2,\dots,x_n,x_0 \rrp \,, \tag*{$\bf (A_2)$}\label{Atranslate} \llp x_0+b,x_1+b,\dots, x_n+b \rrp = \llp x_0,x_1,\dots,x_n \rrp  for $b\in F$, \tag*{$\bf (A_3)$}\label{Alog} \llp 0,x \rrp + \llp 0,y \rrp = \llp 0,xy \rrp\ \text{for $ x,y \in F^{\times}$,} \tag*{$\bf (A_4)$}\label{Ascale} \llp mx_0,mx_1,\dots, mx_n\rrp = \llp x_0,x_1,\dots,x_n\rrp \ \text{for $m\in F^{\times}$ and $n\geq 2$,} \tag*{$\bf (A_5)$}\label{Azero} \llp 0,0,\dots,0 \rrp =0\,, \tag*{$\bf (A_6)$}\label{Aone} \llp 1,0,\dots,0\rrp =0\,.\end{equation}.}

elements.sent-2411-468: Recall the definition of a Lie coalgebra.
elements.sent-2411-469: For a vector space $V$ over $\Q$, consider maps $\tau \colon V \otimes V \lra V \otimes V$ sending $a\otimes b$ to $b\otimes a$ and $\eta \colon V \otimes V \otimes V\lra V \otimes V\otimes V$ sending $a\otimes b \otimes c$ to $b\otimes c \otimes a$.
elements.sent-2411-470: A~Lie coalgebra is a $\Q$-vector space $\L$ together with a map $\delta \colon\L\lra \L\otimes \L$ such that $\tau \circ \delta = -\delta$ and $(1+\eta +\eta^2)\circ (1\otimes \delta)\circ\delta=0$ (coJacobi identity).
elements.sent-2411-471: The subspace $\bigwedge^2 \L\subseteq \L\otimes \L$ is spanned by elements $v_1\wedge v_2 \coloneqq v_1\otimes v_2-v_2 \otimes v_1$; the image of the Lie cobracket is contained in this subspace.
elements.sent-2411-472: We define a Lie cobracket $\delta\colon \mathcal{A (F)\lra \bigwedge^2 \mathcal{A (F)$ by the formula \begin{equation}\delta \llp x_0,\dots, x_n\rrp =\sum_{j=0}^n\sum_{i=1}^{n-1}\llp x_{j}, x_{j+1}, \dots, x_{j+i}\rrp \wedge \llp x_{j}, x_{j+i+1}, \dots, x_{j+n}\rrp \,,\end{equation} where we use the convention that $x_{i+n+1 =x_{i}$ for all $i$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-473: This definition is motivated by the formula for the cobracket of motivic correlators~\cite[Eq.~(66)]{GR-zeta4 .}
